name: Secure Integration Zones
strategicIntent: Establish isolated network and compute zones for hosting third-party integration connectors, ensuring that external partner traffic never reaches internal systems directly and all egress is controlled and auditable.
problemStatement: Third-party connectors (EDI partners, logistics providers, payment gateways) require network connectivity that, if hosted within internal zones, exposes core systems to external attack vectors and complicates compliance with data residency requirements.

useCases.use: EDI connectors for retailer order ingestion (Walmart, Costco, Tesco)
useCases.dontUse: Internal service-to-service integration within the same trust boundary

useCases.use: Payment gateway connectors requiring PCI DSS isolation
useCases.dontUse: Azure-native PaaS integrations using private endpoints within the corporate network

useCases.use: Logistics partner APIs for shipment tracking and carrier booking
useCases.dontUse: Internal BI reporting connections to Azure Synapse or Data Lake

components: Azure Virtual Network (DMZ spoke) — isolated network zone for integration workloads
Azure Firewall — egress filtering and threat inspection for outbound partner traffic
Azure API Management (isolated tier) — API proxy within the integration zone
Azure Private Link — secure inbound connectivity from integration zone to internal services
Azure NSG & ASG — micro-segmentation within the integration zone
Azure Monitor — network flow logging and anomaly detection
interactionSteps: Third-party connector is deployed into the isolated DMZ spoke VNet
Inbound partner traffic is routed through Azure Firewall with allow-listed IPs and FQDNs
Connector processes partner data and transforms to internal canonical format
Outbound calls to internal services routed via Private Link endpoints only
Azure Firewall enforces egress allow-list — blocks all non-approved outbound destinations
Network flow logs capture all traffic for security monitoring and compliance auditing
Anomaly detection alerts on unexpected traffic patterns or volume spikes
securityConsiderations: Integration zone must be a separate VNet with no direct peering to internal application VNets
All egress from the integration zone must pass through Azure Firewall with explicit allow-lists
Third-party connector images must be scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment
Data leaving the integration zone to partners must be encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+)
PCI DSS-scoped connectors must run in a dedicated sub-zone with additional audit controls
No corporate credentials may be stored within the integration zone — use Key Vault references

guardrails.rule: Integration zone VNet must not have direct peering to internal VNets
All egress must be filtered through Azure Firewall with FQDN allow-lists
PCI-scoped connectors must be deployed in dedicated PCI sub-zone
Network flow logs must be retained for 90 days minimum
Third-party container images must pass vulnerability scan before deployment

exceptionProcess: Submit Security Architecture Board request with threat model for any direct connectivity bypassing the integration zone. CISO and Network Team approval required. Must include compensating network controls and 30-day review.

revisionHistory.author: Raj Patel
revisionHistory.summary: Initial secure integration zone pattern with DMZ spoke design

revisionHistory.author: Raj Patel
revisionHistory.summary: Added PCI sub-zone guidance, egress FQDN filtering, and container image scanning
